qt: A table is in third normal form if it is in second normal form and there is no __________.
A relation is in third normal form if ________.
op: It is already in second normal form

qt: Which of the following action is performed on a relation to remove the transitive dependency?
Consider the following relation: STD(stId, stName, stAdr, prName, prCrdts) stId → stName, stAdr, prName, prCrdts prName → prCrdts Which of the following dependency exist in above relation?
The anomalies caused by transitive dependency in a relation are removed by __________.
A transitive dependency occurs when __________.
op: Part of the key is dependent on key attribute
Primary key attribute is dependent on non-key attribute
Non-key attribute is not dependent on another non-key attribute
Non-key attribute is dependent on another non-key attribute

qt: If a relation is not in third normal form, then anomalies are due to ________.

INSERT INTO statement is used to enter values into__________.
Which of the following is the correct syntax for inserting some values into the employee table?
op: INSERT into employee (col1, col2, col3) VALUES (val1, val2, val3)
INSERT into employee (col1, col2, col3), (val1, val2, val3)
INSERT into (col1, col2, col3) VALUES (val1, val2, val3)
INSERT into employee COLUMN (col1, col2, col3) VALUES (val1, val2, val3)

qt: Consider the following two sets: STD Name = [Ali, Hassan] Course Name = {Eng, Math, Phy} Then the Cartesian product of STD Name and Course Name (i.e. STD Name x Course Name) will be equal to:
op: {(Ali, Math), (Ali, Phy), (Hassan, Phy), (Hassan, Eng), (Hassan, Math), (Hassan, Phy)}
{(Ali, Eng), (Ali, Math), (Ali, Phy), (Hassan, Eng), (Hassan, Math), (Hassan, Phy)}
{(Ali, Eng), (Hassan, Math), (Ali, Phy), (Hassan, Eng), (Hassan, Math), (Hassan, Phy)}
{(Ali, Eng), (Ali, Math), (Ali, Phy), (Hassan, Phy), (Ali, Math), (Hassan, Eng)}

op: Do not appear in both X and Y

Appear in both X and Y

op: Primary key consists of single attribute
